verses.text: On the sabbath He asks, is it lawful to heal? They cannot answer. Silenced. Their own consciences could not condemn His mercy.
On the sabbath He asks, is it lawful to heal? They cannot answer. Silenced. Their own consciences could not condemn His mercy.
On the sabbath He asks, is it lawful to heal? They cannot answer. Silenced. Their own consciences could not condemn His mercy.
He heals the man with dropsy; which of you would not pull an ox from a pit on the sabbath? Plain truth. Compassion is never out of season.

Take not the chief seat; 'whosoever exalteth himself shall be abased,' and the humble exalted. Humility honoured. The low place is the way to honour with God.

Bid the poor and lame, who cannot repay; 'thou shalt be recompensed at the resurrection.' Selfless giving. Kindness to the needy is repaid by God.
Bid the poor and lame, who cannot repay; 'thou shalt be recompensed at the resurrection.' Selfless giving. Kindness to the needy is repaid by God.
Bid the poor and lame, who cannot repay; 'thou shalt be recompensed at the resurrection.' Selfless giving. Kindness to the needy is repaid by God.
A man makes a great supper and bids many, 'come; for all things are now ready.' Free invitation. The gospel feast is spread, and all are called.
A man makes a great supper and bids many, 'come; for all things are now ready.' Free invitation. The gospel feast is spread, and all are called.
A man makes a great supper and bids many, 'come; for all things are now ready.' Free invitation. The gospel feast is spread, and all are called.
All make excuse — a field, oxen, a wife. Trifling refusals. Worldly cares keep men from the feast of grace.
All make excuse — a field, oxen, a wife. Trifling refusals. Worldly cares keep men from the feast of grace.
All make excuse — a field, oxen, a wife. Trifling refusals. Worldly cares keep men from the feast of grace.
Angry, the master calls in the poor and compels the highways; none bidden shall taste his supper. Grace to outsiders. When the favoured refuse, others are brought in.

Whoso comes after Me and 'hate not' even his own life cannot be My disciple; he must bear his cross. Christ first. He must hold first place above all.
Whoso comes after Me and 'hate not' even his own life cannot be My disciple; he must bear his cross. Christ first. He must hold first place above all.
Whoso comes after Me and 'hate not' even his own life cannot be My disciple; he must bear his cross. Christ first. He must hold first place above all.
Who builds a tower without counting the cost, lest he be mocked? Forethought. Discipleship must be entered with open eyes.
Who builds a tower without counting the cost, lest he be mocked? Forethought. Discipleship must be entered with open eyes.
Who builds a tower without counting the cost, lest he be mocked? Forethought. Discipleship must be entered with open eyes.
What king goes to war without counting his forces? whoso forsakes not all cannot be My disciple. Total surrender. Following Christ claims everything.

Salt that loses savour is cast out; 'he that hath ears... let him hear.' Useless if tasteless. A disciple without devotion is good for nothing.
